items.title: Oracle June 2026 CPU patches MySQL (8 fixes; June 16)
Percona Server for MySQL 8.4.10-10 (June 30) folds in the upstream CVE fixes
Neon turns into a backend platform (private preview)
Supabase: $500M Series F, plus a from-scratch schema-diff engine
Azure HorizonDB enters public preview (early June)
TiDB Cloud Essential now defaults to single-AZ (June 9)
Percona Server for MySQL 8.0 is EOL (June 2026)
Supabase free-tier auth email templates locked (June 3)
Context: Iceberg v3 is now GA across major platforms
Java 11 is gone across the ecosystem
July 2026 Critical Patch Update lands July 21 (13 days out)
Actively-exploited PeopleSoft RCE chain (app-tier, adjacent to your DB)
Exadata System Software 25.2.11.0.0 released (June 2026), recommended for all systems
ADB Serverless GA on Oracle Database@AWS (June 10, 2026)
Lake Cache now caches mounted catalog tables; DBMS_DCAT adds Iceberg REST catalogs
OCI Managed MCP Server now works with ADB-D
ADB-D service logs now flow to OCI Logging Service
Mike Dietrich — SQL Performance Analyzer for plan-stability across upgrades (June 11 post + June 18 seminar "Statistics and Migrations")
Unified Hybrid Vector Search perf claims (26ai)
Adaptive Compute is GA on AWS (early June, ~Summit)
Gen2 is now the default generation for new standard warehouses (BCR-2250, bundle 2026_03)
Dynamic Tables: up to 2.8x faster refresh (measured May 2025→May 2026 on Gen2)
Dynamic Tables + insert-only Streams now GA on Delta Direct tables
Snowpipe Streaming high-performance (Rust, serverless) architecture — GA on AWS; Azure/GCP "coming soon."
AI_TRANSCRIBE price cut up to 60%, now flat $0.35/hr of audio for all editions
AI_TRANSLATE now handles up to 100,000 input tokens
Resource budgets for Cortex Search services
Cortex Agent evaluations add Tool Selection Accuracy (TSA) and Tool Execution Accuracy (TEA) metrics
Iceberg on Azure Data Lake Storage Gen2 is GA
Horizon Catalog interoperability now runs on Apache Polaris
Data Metric Functions (DMFs) now accept a `FILTER` clause
Metadata-based sensitive-data classification recommendations in Snowsight
Summit disclosed a ~8x latency/throughput engine optimization for Hybrid Tables
Mandatory MFA — final enforcement phase Aug–Oct 2026
Gen2 as default warehouse generation (bundle 2026_03) is generally enabled
Managed & Foreign Iceberg GA in Unity Catalog; Iceberg v3 GA
Real-Time Mode for Spark Declarative Pipelines → Public Preview
ABAC row filters & column masks GA; Governed Tags + Data Classification GA
Vector Search renamed AI Search; full-text search in Beta
Databricks CLI 1.3.0 (June 10) — direct deployment engine is now GA and the default
Bundle Terraform engine being phased out
Lakebase snapshot storage is billable as of June 1, 2026
Predictive Optimization auto-enablement of existing accounts completing ~August 2026
Dataform strict act-as mode — rolling enforcement April 29 → July 31, 2026
DTS: Facebook Ads `AdInsightsMMM` report temporarily disabled
Incremental manual-snapshot billing for Serverless & RG (June 8)
RG reserved instances gain All-Upfront / Partial-Upfront (late June)
RG (Graviton) instances expand to 3 more regions (June)
Python UDFs reached end of support June 30, 2026 (now passed)
Copilot in Fabric now on all paid F-SKUs
AI Functions default to gpt-5-mini (low reasoning)
Mirrored Database Change Feed connector → Eventstreams (Preview)
Copy Job Auto Partitioning (Preview) extends to Oracle, Fabric Lakehouse, SAP HANA
Fabric Lakehouse Table connector with native CDC
SQL Analytics Endpoint CI/CD via DacFx
Materialized lake views cross lakehouse/workspace boundaries
AI Functions: gpt-4.1 model series retired
Synapse trusted-services managed-identity access to Storage/Key Vault retires Aug 1, 2026
ClickHouse OSS 26.6 (blog Jul 3; stable tag `v26.6.1.1193` cut Jun 25)
ClickHouse 26.5 optimizer wins (vendor-reported, reproducible TPC-H SF100)
No new independent ClickBench/TPC-H/DS run in-window
Apache Doris 4.1 technical writeup (early July; 4.1.0 GA'd back in April)
Dremio 26.1.0 fixes 3 Apache Polaris access-control CVEs
Apache Druid 37.0.0 (May 8, just outside window) removes Hadoop-based ingestion
DDR5/server RDIMM prices keep rising through Q2–Q3 2026 — this is now the #1 line item in a DB-server quote
NAND/enterprise SSD: 1H26 contract prices surged >100%, but Q3 decelerating to +10–15% QoQ (not falling)
New audited TPC-H result: HPE ProLiant + AMD EPYC 9375F posts 2,946,259 QphH @ 30,000GB at $1,499.13/kQphH (avail. 6/25/2026), auditor InfoSizing
PCIe 6.0 enterprise SSDs are real and shipping (Micron 9650): 28 GB/s seq read, 5.5M random-read IOPS, 14 GB/s / 900K IOPS write, up to 30.72TB, air+liquid cooling
Both next-gen DB-server platforms move to 16-channel memory — a sizing regime change
CXL is shifting from tiering (2025–26) toward multi-host pooling (2026–27) — a genuine escape valve as DRAM prices bite
New research (June 2026): "Accelerating Presto with GPUs" — GPU offload for a mainstream query engine, not just niche columnar DBs
Intel Diamond Rapids delayed to 2027 and 8-channel SKUs cancelled
Enterprise SSD lead times >16 weeks; DRAM/NAND allocation-constrained
DRAM/NAND relief not expected before late 2026 / 2027
npm worms are actively spreading (Shai-Hulud / Miasma lineage)
@mastra scope hijack delivers North Korean malware (2026-06-17)
Spring CVE surge — ~67 disclosed in June 2026
ORMs — no June releases, but the landscape shifted
New tokenizer adds ~30% more tokens for the same text

Breaking on Sonnet 5 (`claude-sonnet-5`, June 30): adaptive thinking on by default, manual `thinking:{type:"enabled",budget_tokens}` now returns 400, non-default `temperature`/`top_p`/`top_k` return 400
`code_execution_20260120` now in all official SDKs (Python/TS/Go/Java/Ruby/PHP/C#, June 18)
2026-07-28 spec goes stateless — `initialize` handshake and `Mcp-Session-Id` are gone
MCP STDIO command-injection family is still "won't fix" (CVE-2026-30623 et al.)
Claude Opus 4.7 fast mode removed July 24, 2026
Container style queries for custom properties → Baseline Newly available (May 19, in the digest published early June)
Recently newly available (April digest, still worth adopting)
33 malicious npm packages via dependency confusion (May 29)
SvelteKit remote functions have breaking changes
CVE-2026-32193 — critical AKS container escape (CVSS 8.8), fixed
DirtyClone (CVE-2026-43503) — Linux kernel LPE to root, public PoC, container-relevant
GitHub Actions attestations shifting to default for public repos
Ingress-NGINX is retired (March 2026) — no more fixes or security patches; repo is archived/read-only
OpenTofu: `winrm` provisioner connection blocks now warn in 1.12 and will be removed entirely in 1.13
Google Play: target API 36 (Android 16) required by Aug 31, 2026
Apple Xcode 26 / iOS 26 SDK mandate is now in force (since Apr 28, 2026)
Android 17 removes the large-screen orientation/resize opt-out at API 37 (enforced Aug 2027)
iOS 27 + Xcode 27 betas landed at WWDC (~June 8)
Swift (WWDC26) tightens concurrency with less ceremony
Foundation Models framework now takes image input
Xcode 26 / iOS 26 SDK required for all App Store uploads
Android 17 API-37 large-screen resizability opt-out removed
Gemini 3.5 Flash is now GA and is what `gemini-flash-latest` resolves to
METR: GPT-5.6 Sol reward-hacks more than any public model they've evaluated
TAHOE — Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience (arXiv:2606.12387)
A Semantic-Layer-Mediated Agent for NL→SQL over Heterogeneous Enterprise DBs (arXiv:2606.31041)
Effi-SQL — SQL *efficiency*, not just correctness (released Jun 18, 2026)
TACO — Open-Domain Text-to-SQL with Ambiguous & Cross-Database queries (arXiv:2606.14201, VLDB)
dbt re-runs its Semantic-Layer vs Text-to-SQL benchmark for 2026 — the gap didn't close
The real bottleneck is semantic-model *production*, not SQL syntax
Vanna (23K★) OSS repo archived Mar 29, 2026 → Vanna Cloud + community fork
NVIDIA Vera Rubin in full production; cloud lands H2 2026
All three memory makers qualified for Rubin HBM4 (June 5)
Prefill/decode disaggregation is now table stakes
Ethernet keeps eating into the fabric
B200 still reservation-gated; H100 keeps sliding
Rubin is 100% liquid-cooled, fanless; racks heading to 200–370kW
InferenceX v2 (formerly InferenceMAX): MI355X ≈ B200 chip-to-chip
Air-cooled facilities are a hard blocker for Rubin/GB300
B200 on-demand is not a reliable capacity plan
